item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If a person is driving, is it a compulsion to try and crash it into food stores, or confectionary especially when they're running low on bodily fat?

Leaving a few loose ends, but I like this.

Neutral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the idea, but I feel the execution leaves quite a lot to be desired. The tone is a tiny bit iffy in places (such as the use of words in quotation marks: better, surely, to use words which don't need to be in them?) and the description seems a little bit unsubtle. I think it would be much more effective if it wasn't all stated upfront, and was presented first as an autonomous car, with the other effects revealed more slowly.

On a re-read, the execution isn't as bad as I'd thought, so no-vote for now and hoping for a bit of a rewrite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure if it counts as a nitpick or personal thing - but I'm not sure that I like that the picture's context can be easily googled and tied to a real life event.

(Also, that's definitely not a van in the picture. and is in fact a compact car - Specifically a Skoda Octavia).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My biggest question is, when exactly is this thing active? Are there a set of car keys that turn it on, or does it just activate whenever the hell it feels like it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A guy who can lug around forty pounds of beer gut for a mile can probably go a lot farther without.

Also

After testing, SCP-1894 was contained and witnesses were given Class-C amnestics.

I think the scip would've been contained as a precaution, and then testing would go on and reveal the anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasn't as amusing or emotionally evocative as I think the idea could have been.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Objection! Less fat means less weight to move around, which means you'll run faster, farther, do more pushups, etc

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
brb, checking my thin privilege.

(fit probably isn't the right word in this situation, but the simple act of losing fat does increase physical fitness, just nowhere near as much as actual exercise)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how it eats people just so it can knock over (literally) natural food stores. If it was just a car that ran on body fat, it wouldn't be nearly as interesting (not to mention reminiscent of a Freddie movie).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. Less fat does not equate to more fit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1894 was recovered on 9/18/1976, from a farmer's market in Atlanta, GA, USA.

The automobile in your article picture is clearly not a pre-1976 model.

That aside, I like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: how does it make them fit by converting their body fat into fuel? This is rather confusing. You mean Skinnier?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Remember kids, crash course diets are very dangerous. They can mess with your body and make you prone to mood swings or explosive front-end collisions.
